primary_sources.title: Federal Constitution Article 132(1)(d) — public services include the MACC

notes: Successor to Anti-Corruption Agency (1982-2008) and Badan Pencegah Rasuah (1967-1982). Operationally independent only since 2009; chief appointed by YDPA on PM advice (Section 5 of Act 694). Legitimacy contested: tenure-extension pattern under multiple PMs (T4A issue 1021); Azam Baki RM100M civil suit (issue 1961); 2025-2026 cases include ARM chip deal (issue 1990). Reports to a five-member Anti-Corruption Advisory Board chaired by a former senior official.
